chunk_summary: This section outlines a one-page intake and follow-up template for assessing metabolic risk in children, emphasizing standardized fields, cutpoints, and follow-up metrics to ensure comprehensive care and timely referrals.
learning_objectives: Understand the key fields required in a one-page intake and follow-up template for pediatric metabolic risk assessment.
Learn the clinical cutpoints for obesity, lipids, prediabetes, and MASLD in children.
Identify the importance of tracking outcomes beyond weight, including waist trends, BMI slope, and activity goals.
Recognize the role of tiered lab plans and referral triggers in managing pediatric metabolic risk.
key_facts: Obesity cutoffs for Indian children: overweight ≥23, obesity ≥27, class 2 and 3 as 120–140% and ≥140% of the 27 cutoff.
Lipid thresholds: high LDL-C ≥130 mg/dL, high non-HDL-C ≥145 mg/dL, high TG ≥100 mg/dL (ages 0–9) or ≥130 mg/dL (ages 10–19).
Prediabetes and diabetes thresholds: fasting glucose 100–125 mg/dL and ≥126 mg/dL; HbA1c 5.7–6.4% and ≥6.5%.
MASLD significance threshold: ALT >2× sex-specific upper limit.
Follow-up templates should track waist trend, BMI slope, sleep schedule, sugary beverage reduction, and daily activity goals.
Referral triggers include abnormal ALT, fasting lipids, or other risk-specific lab results.
reference_quote: Standardized templates reduce missed comorbidities and make follow-up intervals reproducible across clinicians. (IAP guidelines on childhood obesity, 2023)
document_key_topics: Clinical cutpoints for obesity, lipids, and diabetes
